sec.method: reported endpoint titer (19.9 +/- 2.9 g/L)

growth_records.cite: Hocq et al. A megatransposon drives the adaptation of Thermoanaerobacter kivui to carbon monoxide. Nat Commun (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-59103-8. [PMC12056078]
growth_records.snippet: The maximum growth rate umax reached 0.20 +/- 0.03 h-1 (Td ~ 3.5 h)... on syngas... OD600 of 2.89 +/- 0.72... acetate titers reached... 19.9 +/- 2.9 g L-1. CO and H2 were co-consumed in the syngas condition.
growth_records.notes: Batch bioreactor, syngas CO/H2/CO2/N2 52/24/21/3, 66C, pH 6.4, 600 rpm, 0.05 vvm. umax=0.20+/-0.03 h-1 reported; Td~3.5 h reported (consistent with ln2/0.20=3.47 h). Final OD600 2.89+/-0.72; final acetate titer 19.9+/-2.9 g/L (titer, NOT specific rate). Specific gas uptake/production rates shown graphically (Fig 2c) but not given numerically in text (Source Data only). Chemostat feed used NH4Cl 1 g/L and FeSO4 28.6 mg/L; batch bioreactor used NH4Cl 1 g/L and FeSO4 3 mg/L (per Methods). Trace element solution (per L): 15.00 g nitrilotriacetic acid, 5.00 g MnSO4.H2O, 1.50 g CoCl2.6H2O, 1.80 g ZnSO4.7H2O, 0.10 g CuSO4.5H2O, 0.10 g H3BO3, 0.10 g Na2MoO4.2H2O, 0.33 g NiSO4.6H2O, 0.003 g Na2SeO3.5H2O, 0.004 g Na2WO4.2H2O.

growth_records.cite: Hocq et al. A megatransposon drives the adaptation of Thermoanaerobacter kivui to carbon monoxide. Nat Commun (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-59103-8. [PMC12056078]
growth_records.snippet: umax... 0.23 +/- 0.06 h-1 (Td ~ 3.0 h)... on... CO... OD600 of... 4.85 +/- 0.01 for... 100% CO... acetate titers reached high levels (... 32.5 +/- 7.1 g L-1). Under 100% CO, later stages... show a water-gas shift reaction, resulting in the
growth_records.notes: Batch bioreactor, 100% CO, 66C, pH 6.4, 300 rpm, 0.05 vvm. umax=0.23+/-0.06 h-1 reported; Td~3.0 h reported (ln2/0.23=3.01 h, consistent). Final OD600 4.85+/-0.01; final acetate titer 32.5+/-7.1 g/L (titer, not rate). Specific gas rates in Fig 2d/Source Data only. Abstract quotes umax ~0.25 h-1 as headline for CO-1.

growth_records.notes: Steady-state chemostat => mu = D = 0.10 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.10 = 6.93 h (calculated). All specific rates (mmol gDW-1 h-1) and yields taken verbatim from Table 2. About 4 H2 and 2 CO2 consumed per acetate (172.4/40.8=4.23; 80.3/40.8=1.97). n=3 replicates. Steady biomass 0.07 g/L, acetate 1.60 g/L.

growth_records.snippet: Table 2: Strain CO-1, Gas H2/CO2, D 0.10 h-1; Biomass 0.10 +/- 0.01 g L-1; Acetate 2.32 +/- 0.14 g L-1; qH2 175.2 +/- 14.0; qCO2 89.2 +/- 7.2; qAce 40.8 +/- 1.3 mmol g-1 h-1; YBiomass/S 0.57 +/- 0.05; YAce/Biomass 0.41 +/- 0.01.

CO-1 on H2/CO2 behaves like G-1 (acetate yields similar; ~4 H2 + 2 CO2 per acetate: 175.2/40.8=4.29, 89.2/40.8=2.19). Values verbatim Table 2. sec.method: reported specific rate qAce (14.6 +/- 0.5)
reported as negative qH2 (-16.7 +/- 3.9) = net H2 production via water-gas shift
reported as negative qCO2 (-47.0 +/- 6.0) = net CO2 production via water-gas shift

growth_records.cite: Hocq et al. A megatransposon drives the adaptation of Thermoanaerobacter kivui to carbon monoxide. Nat Commun (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-59103-8. [PMC12056078]
growth_records.notes: Steady-state chemostat => mu = D = 0.10 h-1; td = 6.93 h. On CO, negative qH2 and qCO2 in Table 2 denote NET PRODUCTION (secretion), hence H2 and CO2 moved to secretion_rates as +16.7 and +47.0; only CO is consumed (uptake 80.0). CO/acetate = 80.0/14.6 = 5.48 (paper: ~5.5 vs theoretical 4). High biomass yield (1.26 g/mol) vs H2/CO2 (0.57) attributed to higher ATP yield of carboxydotrophy. Values verbatim Table 2. n=3.

growth_records.cite: Hocq et al. A megatransposon drives the adaptation of Thermoanaerobacter kivui to carbon monoxide. Nat Commun (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-59103-8. [PMC12056078]
growth_records.notes: Steady-state chemostat => mu = D = 0.20 h-1; td = ln2/0.20 = 3.47 h (calculated). CO the sole consumed substrate (qCO 122.2); H2 (-26.1) and CO2 (-75.0) are NET PRODUCED (moved to secretion as +26.1 and +75.0). CO/acetate = 122.2/27.3 = 4.48. Highest biomass yield 1.66 g/mol. Only 2 replicates at this D. Values verbatim Table 2.

up.method: steady-state glucose consumption rate calculated from in situ concentration and flow rate; growth-rate-resolved values plotted in Fig. 5a / Source Data, not given numerically in text

sec.method: steady-state acetate production rate from in situ concentration x flow rate; values in Fig. 5b / Source Data, not numerical in text. Homoacetogen: glucose -> ~3 acetate (2 via EMP glycolysis + PFOR, 1 via Wood-Ljungdahl from 2 CO2)
net CO2 largely re-fixed via WLP; gas phase was open to release pressure so gaseous CO2/H2 production rates were explicitly NOT quantified
H2 monitored by GC but gas formation not quantified (open gas phase)

yields.maintenance_energy: 0.5 mmol ATP gDW-1 h-1 (estimated from slope, Fig. 1c)
yields.ATP_yield: 2.9 mol ATP per 3 mol extracellular acetate
yields.Yxs_note: biomass yield (gDW per mol ATP) correlated positively with growth rate (Fig. 1b); exact Yxs not tabulated in text
yields.product_yield_note: acetate/glucose molar ratio ~3 (homoacetogenic), exact per-condition ratio in Fig. 5c

growth_records.cite: Mueller et al. Non-canonical resource allocation in heterotrophically growing Thermoanaerobacter kivui. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-63432-z. [PMC12475172]
growth_records.snippet: growing heterotrophically in glucose-limited, anoxic chemostats ... spanning two orders of magnitude (0.001 h to 0.101 h-1, equivalent to doubling times of 30 d to 7 h) ... very slow (<0.003 h-1), slow (up to 0.03 h-1), medium (up to 0.04 h
growth_records.notes: Chemostat: at steady state mu = D. This record = slowest end of the studied continuum, D=0.001 h-1 (stated minimum). Reported doubling time 30 d = 720 h (check: ln2/0.001 = 693 h = 28.9 d, consistent). Temp 65 C (all T. kivui cultures cultivated at 65 C). Medium adjusted to pH 7.0 with NaOH but reactor pH monitored stable at 6.0 during steady state -> pH field = 6.0 (reactor). Stirred 300 rpm. Cultures grown >=4 retention times before sampling. Catabolism: glucose -> 2 acetate + 2 CO2 via EMP+PFOR, plus WLP homoacetogenesis (CO2 -> acetate). Specific glucose-uptake and acetate-production rates are growth-rate-dependent and reported graphically (Fig. 5a,b) / Source Data, not as text numbers, so qS/qP left null.

growth_records.medium: MTki buffered minimal medium (glucose-limited, anoxic; 10 mM glucose at highest flow rate)

up.method: steady-state glucose consumption from in situ conc x flow rate; growth-rate-resolved in Fig. 5a / Source Data

sec.method: steady-state acetate production from in situ conc x flow rate; Fig. 5b / Source Data. Homoacetogenic glucose -> ~3 acetate
gas phase open, gaseous CO2 production rate not quantified
gas formation not quantified

yields.ATP_yield: 2.9 mol ATP per 3 mol extracellular acetate
yields.product_yield_note: acetate/glucose molar ratio ~3, per-condition value in Fig. 5c

growth_records.cite: Mueller et al. Non-canonical resource allocation in heterotrophically growing Thermoanaerobacter kivui. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-63432-z. [PMC12475172]
growth_records.snippet: spanning two orders of magnitude (0.001 h to 0.101 h-1, equivalent to doubling times of 30 d to 7 h) ... fast-growing (>0.04 h-1) chemostat cultures ... chemostats at the highest flow rate (>0.05 h-1, setting 2 rpm) were cultivated using me
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu = D. This record = fastest end of the studied continuum, D=0.101 h-1 (stated maximum). Reported doubling time 7 h (check: ln2/0.101 = 6.86 h, consistent). Highest-flow chemostats used 10 mM (not 20 mM) glucose. Temp 65 C. Reactor pH ~6.0 (medium set pH 7.0). qS/qP reported graphically (Fig. 5a,b), not numerically in text, so left null. The full study covers a continuum of dilution rates 0.001-0.101 h-1 grouped as very slow (<0.003), slow (<=0.03), medium (<=0.04), fast (>0.04); only the two stated numeric endpoints are captured as discrete records.

sec.method: homoacetogenic product, glucose -> ~3 acetate (EMP+PFOR + Wood-Ljungdahl); specific rate not tabulated in text
net CO2 largely re-fixed via WLP; not quantified as specific rate

yields.ATP_yield: 2.9 mol ATP per 3 mol extracellular acetate

growth_records.cite: Mueller et al. Non-canonical resource allocation in heterotrophically growing Thermoanaerobacter kivui. Nature Communications (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41467-025-63432-z. [PMC12475172]
growth_records.snippet: in comparison to exponentially growing batch cultures (0.173 h-1). Before systems-level measurements, chemostat cultures were grown for at least four doubling times
growth_records.notes: Batch exponential mu = 0.173 h-1 (reported). Doubling time CALCULATED: td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.173 = 4.006 h ~ 4.01 h. Temp 65 C, anaerobic, MTki medium with 20 mM glucose, pH set 7.0. Growth on glucose as sole C/energy source via homoacetogenesis (glucose -> ~3 acetate). Specific qS/qP not reported numerically in text (figure/Source Data).
